context: Tests whether the agent implements duties estimation using EasyPost, defines the correct DutyEstimation interface, uses accurate de minimis thresholds per country, suppresses estimates for below-threshold orders, and implements a graceful fallback to DDU rather than throwing on API failure.

checklist.name: EasyPost package
checklist.description: Imports and uses '@easypost/api' package (not an alternative duties/tax API like taxjar, avalara, stripe, or a generic HTTP client)

checklist.name: DutyEstimation method type
checklist.description: DutyEstimation interface declares 'method' as the union type 'DDP' | 'DDU' (not just string)

checklist.name: Fallback returns DDU
checklist.description: When the EasyPost API call fails (catch block), the function returns a result with method set to 'DDU' rather than throwing or returning DDP

checklist.name: Fallback GB VAT rate
checklist.description: Fallback VAT rate for GB is exactly 0.20 (20%)

checklist.name: Fallback DE VAT rate
checklist.description: Fallback VAT rate for DE is exactly 0.19 (19%)

checklist.name: US de minimis threshold
checklist.description: DE_MINIMIS_CENTS includes US mapped to 80000 (representing $800 USD)

checklist.name: De minimis suppression
checklist.description: checkout-integration.md or code shows that duty estimate is suppressed (not shown / set to zero) when the order total is at or below the destination country de minimis threshold

checklist.name: Monetary values in cents
checklist.description: DutyEstimation interface uses integer cents for duties, taxes, and total fields (not floats/dollars)

checklist.name: EasyPost API key from env
checklist.description: EasyPost client is instantiated using process.env.EASYPOST_API_KEY (not a hardcoded key)

checklist.name: Fallback no-throw
checklist.description: The catch block does NOT re-throw the error — execution continues to return a fallback DDU estimate
